title: hackelia hendersonii
intro: Hackelia hendersonii, also known as Henderson's stickseed, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). This perennial plant is native to the southwestern United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Hackelia hendersonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and infections.
